rationale: The archetype of Meridian's thesis: a clinical insider (5 years NHS) with a shipped AI prototype, a major European hackathon win, and pull from real hospital buyers — all pre-institutional. Check-size fit is clean at pre-seed.

Second-time founder with live users and published retention in European digital health matches the thesis almost exactly. Slight discount for hardware-adjacency: device partnerships could stretch beyond a pre-seed software motion.

Clinician-founder authenticity and a regulatory-aware European imaging wedge fit the thesis well. Discounted for solo-founder risk and an unpaid pilot pipeline — classic pre-seed diligence questions rather than disqualifiers.

Nordic hospital-operations software from a buyer-side operator de-risks the sales motion Meridian cares about. Earlier than the sweet spot: no public launch or revenue motion yet — a strong tracking candidate.

Ticks the AI + healthcare + Europe boxes with unusual hardware depth, but CE-marking timelines and manufacturing capital sit outside Meridian's typical pre-seed software motion. Watch-list ahead of clinical pilot data.

The only verified revenue in this set, in-thesis geography and domain — but she indexes off the pre-seed venture profile: bootstrapped, possibly not raising, and publicly oriented toward sustainability over venture scale.

Hospital-flow simulation fits the thesis on paper and the research depth is rare, but the score is suppressed by verification risk: a 0.62 identity match and an inferred, unconfirmed hospital conversation. Verifies-up into the high 70s if diligence clears.
